snippets.title: Boris Cherny on coding and models
snippets.takeaway: As engineering, product, design, DS, etc. melt into a new kind of role, I was reflecting on what roles might look like in the future.
snippets.summary: Boris Cherny (bcherny on X) shared 1 recent update. As engineering, product, design, DS, etc. melt into a new kind of role, I was reflecting on what roles might look like in the future. For example, when I look at the Claude Code team I see what I think is five archetypes: 1. Prototyper: comes up with brand new ideas; churns out many ideas, most of which don't ship 2. Builder: quickly turns a prototype/idea into production-grade product/infra 3.

snippets.title: Thibault Sottiaux on coding and tools
snippets.takeaway: As we are still investigating, I have reset everyone's Codex usage limits. This is a hard reset given some users had stacked up to three banked resets already that they can apply...
snippets.summary: Thibault Sottiaux (thsottiaux on X) shared 3 recent updates. As we are still investigating, I have reset everyone's Codex usage limits. This is a hard reset given some users had stacked up to three banked resets already that they can apply on their own schedule. Funnily enough, this week at OpenAI is called the RESET week, which is meant for folks to relax a bit. However it will be a different kind of RESET week. Enjoy.

snippets.title: Peter Yang on agents and coding

snippets.summary: Peter Yang (petergyang on X) shared 3 recent updates. How Anthropic PMs use agents internally to get closer to the product from Jess, product lead for Claude Managed Agents: “Access to our codebase has been the biggest unlock for me. It helps me manage state more easily. Rather than poking a bunch of engineers on what they’re doing, I can just track the PRs directly and see which ones are merged, which ones are deployed.

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on models and research
snippets.takeaway: It should be 100% obvious that there will soon be mythos level models on cyber security that are open and available to anyone.
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 1 recent update. It should be 100% obvious that there will soon be mythos level models on cyber security that are open and available to anyone. As a byproduct of this, alternative tech stacks will emerge that also drive more economic value and control away from the US’s tech stack. This is what should be considered when thinking through the gate keeping you want to have in AI.

snippets.title: How we contain Claude across products
snippets.takeaway: How we contain Claude across products. Twelve months ago, we'd have rejected out of hand the idea of granting Claude access sufficient to take down an internal Anthropic...
snippets.summary: How we contain Claude across products. Twelve months ago, we'd have rejected out of hand the idea of granting Claude access sufficient to take down an internal Anthropic service. Today that level of access is routine, and Anthropic developers are more productive for it. The risk of these deployments has two components: how likely a failure is, and how much damage one could do.

snippets.takeaway: The GPU Myth: State of AI Compute 2026 | Stephen Balaban. Maybe it's a long running agent workflow. I think that that just means that everybody is able to process 10 times more...
snippets.summary: Maybe it's a long running agent workflow. I think that that just means that everybody is able to process 10 times more tokens, and there's there's still the same fixed amount of compute in the world at any given point in time. Speaker 1 | 1:06:17 - 1:06:35 Well, I mean, a lot of the engineers at Lambda are already, you know, doing a fully agent driven workflow.
